stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Coastal estuaries provide a steady supply of small fish, crabs, and shrimp.
  • This abundant prey supports the development of larger, healthier offspring for shorebirds that nest near estuaries.
  • On average, 78 percent of shorebird chicks raised near coastal estuaries reach adulthood.
  • Inland wetlands are dominated by a narrower range of insect prey.
  • On average, only 45 percent of shorebird chicks raised at inland wetlands reach adulthood.
stem: The student wants to make and support a generalization about shorebirds. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Coastal estuaries offer a wider range of prey than do inland wetlands, which are dominated by insects.
Near coastal estuaries, 78 percent of shorebird chicks reach adulthood, whereas at inland wetlands, only 45 percent do.
Shorebirds are more likely to thrive near coastal estuaries than at inland wetlands because the varied prey available in estuaries supports healthier offspring.
The development of shorebird chicks depends on what they eat, and abundant prey is more available near coastal estuaries than at inland wetlands.

rationale:
Choice C is the best answer because the sentence makes and supports a generalization about shorebirds. It claims that shorebirds are more likely to thrive near coastal estuaries than at inland wetlands, and it supports the claim with information about the effect of varied prey on chick development.

Choice A is incorrect. While the sentence makes a generalization, it doesn’t mention shorebirds. Choice B is incorrect. While the sentence provides survival data, it doesn’t make a generalization about shorebirds based on this information. Choice D is incorrect. While the sentence makes a generalization about shorebirds’ diets and chick development, it doesn’t provide adequate support for this generalization.
